events.headline: Iran threatens Hormuz oil shipping route
events.context: Iran warned it may block the world's most important oil corridor — 20% of global oil ships through here daily. Threat alone moves markets.
events.whyItMoves: Oil shipping at risk → investors sell shares, rush to gold (safe haven). Defense companies rise on expected military activity.

historicalPattern.label: Last time this happened
historicalPattern.detail: June 2019 Hormuz threat — gold rose 5.2% over 2 weeks, oil 9% in 5 days.

events.headline: Fed signals one more rate cut in December
events.context: US central bank hinted it may lower interest rates one more time before year-end. Lower rates = cheaper borrowing.
events.whyItMoves: Lower rates = cheaper money = companies grow faster + people spend more. Tech and growth stocks love this. Dollar weakens.

historicalPattern.label: Pattern history
historicalPattern.detail: Last 4 rate cuts: Nasdaq averaged +3% next month. Cuts during recessions saw stocks fall first.

events.headline: Nvidia smashes Q1, unveils new AI chip
events.context: Nvidia, maker of chips powering most AI today, reported record sales and unveiled a faster Blackwell-2 chip.
events.whyItMoves: When AI leader wins, money flows into anything AI-related. Suppliers, cloud providers, even power utilities benefit.

historicalPattern.label: Sector ripple
historicalPattern.detail: Past 6 NVDA beats: AI ETFs averaged +2.5% next day. Power utilities now 0.6 correlated to NVDA.

events.headline: RBA holds cash rate at 4.10%, signals data-dependent
events.context: Reserve Bank of Australia kept rates unchanged. Says future moves depend on inflation and jobs data.
events.whyItMoves: When rates hold, banks earn more on loans (good for AU big-4). AUD steady. Mortgage holders relieved.

historicalPattern.label: Past holds
historicalPattern.detail: AU big-4 banks averaged +0.8% in week after RBA holds.

events.headline: Iron ore drops 4% on weak China construction data
events.context: China property starts fell again — bad for iron ore demand. Australia's biggest miners feel it first.
events.whyItMoves: China builds with Aussie iron ore. Less Chinese building = less iron demand = AU miners' margins shrink.

historicalPattern.label: Pattern check
historicalPattern.detail: Iron ore -4% days have averaged BHP -1.6% historically.
